refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve a measured p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target for the load→travel→dump cycle under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, with cycle time performance monitored and reported via telemetry.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-002 is a key performance indicator (KPI) requirement that directly impacts customer productivity and system acceptance. Refinement adds explicit reference to measurement/monitoring (telemetry) and duty cycle context to make verification concrete. High safety criticality tailoring requires that cycle time optimization does not compromise safety interlocks or emergency response.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Instrumented field trials under representative duty cycle; p95 cycle time computed from telemetry logs; comparison against CT_target acceptance threshold; safety event correlation analysis to ensure no safety compromise.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-002.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump sub-cycles with deterministic timing within design envelope.
FR-NGHE-002.2: The teleoperation system shall introduce latency and control overhead not exceeding allocated cycle time budget.
FR-NGHE-002.3: Safety services shall not introduce blocking delays that degrade cycle time below target, except during safety-critical interventions.
FR-NGHE-002.4: The NGHE solution shall continuously monitor and report cycle time metrics via telemetry for performance tracking.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Optimize vehicle hydraulic/mechanical actuation timing; minimize travel path via route planning; parallelize load/dump operations where safe.
DP-B: Implement predictive cycle time modeling with real-time adjustment of operational parameters (speed, acceleration profiles) based on site conditions and duty cycle.
DP-C: Establish teleoperation control architecture with low-latency network link and deterministic command processing; allocate cycle time budget across vehicle, teleoperation, and safety subsystems.
DP-D: Integrate safety services as non-blocking advisory layer; use safety alerts to inform operator/tele-operator of constraints without hard stops unless imminent hazard detected.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-C + DP-D (integrated): Establish a deterministic teleoperation control architecture with explicit cycle time budget allocation across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems. Safety services operate as a non-blocking advisory layer (SafetyAlert interface) that informs operator decisions without introducing unnecessary latency, except during imminent hazard detection. Telemetry continuously reports cycle time metrics to enable performance tracking and duty cycle validation.

potential_sources: Coupling between Teleoperation latency and Vehicle actuation timing: mitigated by explicit budget allocation and Network Link interface specification.
Coupling between Safety Services blocking behavior and cycle time: mitigated by non-blocking advisory design pattern (SafetyAlert) with exception for imminent hazard.
Coupling between Telemetry reporting overhead and cycle time: mitigated by asynchronous telemetry collection (does not block operational cycle).
coupling_check.notes: Selected DP avoids tight coupling by separating concerns: Vehicle handles mechanical timing, Teleoperation handles command latency, Safety Services handles advisory/intervention logic. Interfaces (Tasking, SafetyAlert, Telemetry, Network Link) provide decoupling boundaries.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: CT_target is achievable within vehicle mechanical/hydraulic design envelope and site conditions (e.g., soil bearing, visibility).
Assumption: Network Link latency is predictable and within teleoperation control budget (typically <500ms round-trip for heavy equipment).
Assumption: Safety Services can distinguish between advisory alerts (non-blocking) and imminent hazard interventions (blocking) with high confidence.
Risk: If site conditions (mud, slope, obstacles) degrade vehicle performance, cycle time may exceed target despite optimized control architecture.
Risk: Tele-operator skill/fatigue may introduce variability in cycle time execution; training and interface design critical.
Risk: Safety Services false positives could trigger unnecessary blocking, degrading cycle time; requires high specificity in hazard detection.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-C to explicitly specify Network Link latency budget (e.g., ≤200ms round-trip) and Teleoperation command processing determinism (e.g., fixed-rate control loop at 50Hz). Refine DP-D to define SafetyAlert classification thresholds (advisory vs. blocking) with quantified confidence levels. Add DP-B (predictive modeling) as a secondary optimization layer to adapt cycle time targets based on real-time site conditions.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Top-level system integration module responsible for orchestrating Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems to achieve cycle time KPI. Owns cycle time budget allocation, telemetry aggregation, and duty cycle validation logic.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution emits cycle time metrics (load start, travel start, dump start, cycle end timestamps; p95 aggregates) to Site Manager and System Engineer for performance tracking and acceptance validation.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives operational tasking (load location, dump location, duty cycle parameters) from Site Manager; translates to Vehicle and Teleoperation sub-tasks.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives safety alerts from Safety Services (advisory and blocking); uses advisory alerts to inform cycle time optimization; respects blocking alerts to halt operations.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution manages Network Link to Teleoperation subsystem; ensures latency budget compliance and command/telemetry synchronization.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highest containment: NGHE Solution is the system-level integration point that owns cycle time KPI, budget allocation, and telemetry aggregation. All sub-functions (Vehicle timing, Teleoperation latency, Safety advisory) are logically contained within NGHE Solution's orchestration scope. Minimal diffusion: only external interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) cross boundary.

Partial containment: Vehicle owns mechanical/hydraulic actuation timing for load/travel/dump sub-cycles, but cycle time KPI is a system-level metric that depends on Teleoperation and Safety Services. Diffusion: Vehicle must expose timing interfaces to NGHE Solution for budget tracking; cycle time optimization may require Vehicle parameter tuning (speed, acceleration) driven by NGHE Solution logic.

Moderate-high containment: Teleoperation owns command latency and control loop determinism, which are critical cycle time contributors. Diffusion: Teleoperation depends on Network Link quality (external) and must coordinate with Vehicle actuation timing (owned by Vehicle module). SafetyAlert integration (from Safety Services) may introduce latency variability.

Partial containment: Safety Services owns hazard detection and alert generation (SafetyAlert interface), but cycle time impact is indirect (advisory vs. blocking behavior). Diffusion: Safety Services must integrate with Vehicle sensors, Teleoperation control, and NGHE Solution orchestration to avoid unnecessary blocking. High diffusion risk if safety logic is scattered across multiple modules.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Cycle time KPI is a system-level emergent property that arises from the interaction of Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. NGHE Solution should own the cycle time budget allocation logic, telemetry aggregation, and duty cycle validation. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services own their respective sub-functions but must expose timing/latency interfaces to NGHE Solution for orchestration and monitoring.
containment_risks: Risk: If cycle time budget allocation logic is not centralized in NGHE Solution, Vehicle and Teleoperation may optimize independently, leading to sub-optimal system performance or conflicting constraints.
Risk: If telemetry aggregation is distributed across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services, p95 cycle time computation may be inconsistent or delayed, hindering acceptance validation.
Risk: If Safety Services blocking behavior is not coordinated with NGHE Solution, safety interventions may degrade cycle time unpredictably, violating KPI or safety requirements.
Risk: Network Link latency variability may not be visible to NGHE Solution if Teleoperation does not expose latency metrics; cycle time optimization becomes reactive rather than predictive.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Establish explicit cycle time budget allocation interface between NGHE Solution and Vehicle/Teleoperation (e.g., DP_CycleTimeBudget: {vehicle_budget_ms, teleoperation_budget_ms, safety_margin_ms}). Require Vehicle and Teleoperation to expose timing telemetry (sub-cycle timestamps, latency measurements) to NGHE Solution via Telemetry interface. Define SafetyAlert classification with quantified latency impact (advisory: 0ms, blocking: immediate halt). Implement NGHE Solution telemetry aggregation logic to compute p95 cycle time and flag budget violations for Site Manager escalation.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP (deterministic teleoperation control architecture with non-blocking safety advisory layer) directly addresses FR (achieve p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target). DP specifies how to allocate cycle time budget across subsystems, minimize teleoperation latency, and prevent safety services from introducing unnecessary delays. Coupling check passes: interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) provide decoupling boundaries. Assumptions and risks are documented and manageable with recommended revisions.

diffusion_risks: Diffusion Risk 1: Cycle time budget allocation logic may be scattered across NGHE Solution, Vehicle, and Teleoperation if ownership boundaries are not clearly defined. Mitigation: Establish explicit budget allocation interface owned by NGHE Solution.

Diffusion Risk 2: Telemetry aggregation for p95 cycle time computation may be duplicated across modules if not centralized. Mitigation: Require all timing data to flow through NGHE Solution Telemetry interface.

Diffusion Risk 3: Safety Services blocking behavior may be implemented inconsistently across Vehicle and Teleoperation if not coordinated. Mitigation: Define SafetyAlert classification and latency impact in NGHE Solution orchestration logic.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution achieves 95% containment for cycle time KPI orchestration. Vehicle (60%), Teleoperation (70%), and Safety Services (50%) own sub-functions but must expose interfaces to NGHE Solution. Recommended revisions focus on centralizing budget allocation, telemetry aggregation, and safety coordination in NGHE Solution to minimize diffusion.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: High: Cycle time KPI is directly measurable via telemetry (timestamps for load, travel, dump sub-cycles). p95 computation is straightforward statistical analysis. Duty cycle validation can be performed against agreed site conditions. Safety event correlation analysis can verify that safety services do not degrade cycle time below target.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear: NGHE Solution owns cycle time KPI and budget allocation. Vehicle owns mechanical timing. Teleoperation owns command latency. Safety Services owns advisory/blocking logic. Interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) provide allocation boundaries. Recommended revisions add explicit budget allocation and telemetry aggregation interfaces to improve clarity.

recommended_revisions: Revision 1 (DP): Specify Network Link latency budget (e.g., ≤200ms round-trip) and Teleoperation control loop determinism (e.g., 50Hz fixed-rate). Add predictive cycle time modeling as secondary optimization layer.

Revision 2 (IM): Establish explicit cycle time budget allocation interface (DP_CycleTimeBudget) owned by NGHE Solution. Require Vehicle and Teleoperation to expose timing telemetry (sub-cycle timestamps, latency measurements).

Revision 3 (IM): Define SafetyAlert classification with quantified latency impact (advisory: 0ms, blocking: immediate halt). Implement NGHE Solution telemetry aggregation logic to compute p95 cycle time and flag budget violations.

Revision 4 (FR): Add explicit verification criteria: p95 cycle time measured from telemetry logs; comparison against CT_target; safety event correlation analysis; duty cycle validation against agreed site conditions.

decision.justification: FR-NGHE-002 is a system-level KPI that requires decomposition into sub-functions (Vehicle timing, Teleoperation latency, Safety advisory, Telemetry aggregation). Selected DP and assigned IM (NGHE Solution) provide a sound architectural foundation, but the four sub-FRs identified in refined_fr require detailed design at the next level. Recommended revisions to DP and IM are feasible and do not require re-work at current level; they inform next-level decomposition. High safety criticality tailoring requires explicit verification of safety/cycle time trade-offs at sub-function level.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-002.1: The vehicle shall execute load, travel, and dump sub-cycles with deterministic timing within design envelope (e.g., load time ≤ T_load, travel time ≤ T_travel, dump time ≤ T_dump).

FR-NGHE-002.2: The teleoperation system shall introduce latency and control overhead not exceeding allocated cycle time budget (e.g., ≤200ms round-trip Network Link latency; ≤50ms Teleoperation command processing).

FR-NGHE-002.3: Safety services shall not introduce blocking delays that degrade cycle time below target, except during safety-critical interventions (e.g., imminent hazard detection with >95% confidence).

FR-NGHE-002.4: The NGHE solution shall continuously monitor and report cycle time metrics via telemetry for performance tracking (e.g., p95 cycle time, sub-cycle timestamps, safety event correlation).

revise.why: Selected DP requires refinement to specify Network Link latency budget, Teleoperation control loop determinism, SafetyAlert classification thresholds, and predictive cycle time modeling. These refinements are necessary to guide Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services sub-function design at next level.

assumptions: CT_target is achievable within vehicle mechanical/hydraulic design envelope and site conditions.
Network Link latency is predictable and within teleoperation control budget (typically <500ms round-trip).
Safety Services can distinguish between advisory alerts (non-blocking) and imminent hazard interventions (blocking) with high confidence (>95%).
Tele-operator skill and training are sufficient to execute cycle time targets consistently.
Telemetry collection and p95 computation do not introduce significant overhead (<5% cycle time impact).
open_risks: Risk: Site conditions (mud, slope, obstacles) may degrade vehicle performance, causing cycle time to exceed target despite optimized control architecture. Mitigation: Implement predictive cycle time modeling to adapt targets based on real-time site conditions.

Risk: Tele-operator skill/fatigue may introduce variability in cycle time execution. Mitigation: Implement operator assistance features (e.g., automated path planning, speed optimization) and training program.

Risk: Safety Services false positives could trigger unnecessary blocking, degrading cycle time. Mitigation: Implement high-specificity hazard detection (>95% confidence) and advisory-first design pattern.

Risk: Network Link latency variability may exceed budget, causing teleoperation control instability. Mitigation: Implement latency monitoring and adaptive control (e.g., predictive command buffering).
Risk: Cycle time budget allocation may be sub-optimal if not validated against real-world duty cycle. Mitigation: Perform instrumented field trials and iteratively refine budget allocation.
evidence_needed: Vehicle mechanical/hydraulic timing specifications (load, travel, dump sub-cycle durations).
Network Link latency measurements (round-trip, jitter) under representative site conditions.
Safety Services hazard detection specifications (detection types, confidence levels, latency impact).
Teleoperation control loop design (command processing rate, latency budget allocation).
Duty cycle specifications (load mass, travel distance, terrain, visibility, operator skill level).
Field trial data: p95 cycle time measurements, safety event logs, telemetry traces, operator feedback.
Acceptance criteria validation: p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target under agreed duty cycle and site conditions.
